title: CLAUDE.md
text: file provides guidance Claude Code (claude.ai/code) working code repository.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: duckknit R package provides knitr chunk engine DuckDB’s CLI. Users write duckdb chunks Rmd/qmd documents targeting database files, persistent CLI sessions across chunks.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: R/session.R — Session registry (.sessions, .session_dbs, .state environments) processx process lifecycle R/exec.R — SQL execution .print-based sentinel detection output capture R/engine.R — knitr engine function (eng_duckdb) chunk option handling, session resolution, ANSI processing R/sitrep.R — duckdb_sitrep() diagnostic function R/zzz.R — .onLoad (engine registration) .onUnload (session cleanup)

title: CLAUDE.md
text: session persistent processx::process running duckdb <db>. Commands sent via stdin; output captured appending .print <unique_sentinel> chunk’s SQL reading stdout sentinel appears. .print command outputs raw text regardless current output mode, never interferes user formatting settings. Sentinel detection uses grep (substring match) rather exact match DuckDB commands like .tables emit ANSI escape codes can wrap sentinel line.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: engine resolves session use via resolve_session() priority order: session option provided → use/create named session db option provided (session) → find existing session db via find_session_by_db(), create new auto-named one (session-1, session-2, etc.) Neither provided → reuse .state$last_session, create first auto-named session Every chunk execution updates .state$last_session. counter last-session state reset duckknit_kill_all_sessions().

title: CLAUDE.md
text: DuckDB’s single-writer file lock prevents multiple CLI processes opening database file. duckknit_start_session() checks .session_dbs launching new process errors immediately another live session holds file. -memory (:memory:) sessions exempt.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: norm_db_path() normalizes database file paths resolving parent directory (always exists) appending filename. handles macOS /var → /private/var symlink issue normalizePath() produces different results depending whether file exists yet.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: DuckDB dot-commands (.tables, .schema, etc.) emit ANSI color codes. ansi chunk option controls handling: FALSE (default) strips , TRUE preserves , "html" converts via cli::ansi_html(). Stripping happens engine duckknit_exec() returns, exec .

title: CLAUDE.md
text: DuckDB errors go stderr; valid output goes stdout. sentinel always appears stdout even errors. default, errors stop document rendering (stop()). error: true, error text included output instead.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Note: snapshot tests require NOT_CRAN=true run. qmd render snapshot test requires package installed (just load_all()), quarto spawns child R process.
code: # Document (regenerate NAMESPACE and man pages) Rscript -e "devtools::document()"  # Build and check Rscript -e "devtools::check()"  # Install locally Rscript -e "devtools::install()"  # Run tests (once tests exist) Rscript -e "devtools::test()"  # Run a single test file Rscript -e "testthat::test_file('tests/testthat/test-<name>.R')"  # Render README quarto render README.qmd  # Build pkgdown site Rscript -e "pkgdown::build_site()"

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Use = assignment, <- functions packages: add package DESCRIPTION Imports use pkg::fun() syntax rather @importFrom Minimize inline comments; comment explain ,

what: A Knitr Engine for DuckDB CLI
title: A Knitr Engine for DuckDB CLI
text: duckknit provides knitr chunk engine interfaces DuckDB command-line interface. allows write duckdb code chunks R Markdown Quarto documents execute SQL DuckDB databases, persistent sessions carry state across chunks.

title: A Knitr Engine for DuckDB CLI
text: DuckDB CLI must installed available PATH. See DuckDB installation guide instructions. can verify installation :
code: duckdb_sitrep() #> duckknit situation report #> ======================== #>  #> DuckDB CLI #>   Path:    /opt/homebrew/bin/duckdb  #>   Version: v1.5.1 (Variegata) 7dbb2e646f  #>  #> Sessions: 0 active

title: A Knitr Engine for DuckDB CLI
text: can install development version duckknit GitHub :

title: A Knitr Engine for DuckDB CLI
text: Load package register duckdb knitr engine: use duckdb chunks document. default, chunks use -memory database chunk reuses last active session, state persists automatically:
code: library(duckknit) ```{duckdb} CREATE TABLE penguins (species TEXT, island TEXT, bill_length DOUBLE); INSERT INTO penguins VALUES   ('Adelie',    'Torgersen', 39.1),   ('Gentoo',    'Biscoe',    47.3),   ('Chinstrap', 'Dream',     46.5); ``` ```{duckdb} SELECT * FROM penguins ORDER BY bill_length DESC; ``` #> ┌───────────┬───────────┬─────────────┐ #> │  species  │  island   │ bill_length │ #> │  varchar  │  varchar  │   double    │ #> ├───────────┼───────────┼─────────────┤ #> │ Gentoo    │ Biscoe    │        47.3 │ #> │ Chinstrap │ Dream     │        46.5 │ #> │ Adelie    │ Torgersen │        39.1 │ #> └───────────┴───────────┴─────────────┘

title: A Knitr Engine for DuckDB CLI
text: Use db chunk option target specific database file. new session created automatically subsequent chunks without options continue using :
code: ```{duckdb} #| db: my.duckdb CREATE TABLE scores (name TEXT, score INT); INSERT INTO scores VALUES ('Alice', 95), ('Bob', 87); ``` ```{duckdb} .tables ``` #>  ───── my ──────  #>  ──── main ─────  #> ┌───────────────┐ #> │    scores     │ #> │               │ #> │ name  varchar │ #> │ score integer │ #> │               │ #> │    2 rows     │ #> └───────────────┘ ```{duckdb} SELECT * FROM scores; ``` #> ┌─────────┬───────┐ #> │  name   │ score │ #> │ varchar │ int32 │ #> ├─────────┼───────┤ #> │ Alice   │    95 │ #> │ Bob     │    87 │ #> └─────────┴───────┘

title: A Knitr Engine for DuckDB CLI
text: Use mode chunk option .mode dot-command control output formatting. Mode changes made .mode persist remainder session:
code: ```{duckdb} #| mode: csv SELECT * FROM scores; ``` #> name,score #> Alice,95 #> Bob,87 ```{duckdb} .mode markdown SELECT * FROM scores; ``` #> | name  | score | #> |-------|------:| #> | Alice | 95    | #> | Bob   | 87    |

title: A Knitr Engine for DuckDB CLI
text: session option lets give session explicit name. useful switching sessions creating independent workspaces:
code: ```{duckdb} #| session: analysis SELECT 42 as answer; ``` #> ┌────────┐ #> │ answer │ #> │ int32  │ #> ├────────┤ #> │     42 │ #> └────────┘ ```{duckdb} #| session: analysis SELECT 'still here' as status; ``` #> ┌────────────┐ #> │   status   │ #> │  varchar   │ #> ├────────────┤ #> │ still here │ #> └────────────┘

title: A Knitr Engine for DuckDB CLI
text: Use duckdb_sitrep() see active sessions. * marks last used session: Use duckknit_list_sessions() get tibble sessions: can also kill individual sessions sessions :
code: ```{r} duckdb_sitrep() ``` #> duckknit situation report #> ======================== #>  #> DuckDB CLI #>   Path:    /opt/homebrew/bin/duckdb  #>   Version: v1.5.1 (Variegata) 7dbb2e646f  #>  #> Sessions: 3 active #>   - analysis (running) -> :memory:  *  #>   - session-1 (running) -> :memory:   #>   - session-2 (running) -> /Users/rundel/Desktop/Projects/duckknit/my.duckdb duckknit_list_sessions() #> # A tibble: 3 × 4 #>   session   status  db                                                active #>   <chr>     <chr>   <chr>                                             <lgl>  #> 1 analysis  running :memory:                                          TRUE   #> 2 session-1 running :memory:                                          FALSE  #> 3 session-2 running /Users/rundel/Desktop/Projects/duckknit/my.duckdb FALSE duckknit_kill_session("analysis") duckknit_kill_all_sessions()

title: A Knitr Engine for DuckDB CLI
text: Standard knitr options (echo, eval, results, include, error) also supported.

text: Knitr engine DuckDB CLI

title: Knitr engine for DuckDB CLI — eng_duckdb
Knitr engine for DuckDB CLI — eng_duckdb
text: options list chunk options provided knitr.

title: Knitr engine for DuckDB CLI — eng_duckdb
text: character string formatted output knitr.

title: Knitr engine for DuckDB CLI — eng_duckdb
text: Supported chunk options: db Path DuckDB database file. Defaults ":memory:". session Session name. already exists, reused. Otherwise new session created. omitted, last used session reused (new one created automatically). mode DuckDB output mode (e.g., "table", "markdown", "csv"). Sends .mode <value> executing chunk's SQL. timeout Maximum milliseconds wait output. Defaults 30000. ansi Controls handling ANSI escape codes output. TRUE preserves , FALSE (default) strips , "html" converts HTML markup via cli::ansi_html().

title: duckknit 0.0.9.9000
text: Initial release. duckdb knitr engine persistent CLI sessions across chunks. Chunk options: db, session, mode, timeout, ansi. duckdb_sitrep() diagnostics.
